item.message: Skill descriptions were shortened to fit the 2% skills context budget. Codex can still see every skill, but some descriptions are shorter. Disable unused skills or plugins to leave more room for the rest.

item.text: I’ll inspect the three schema/model/query files, update the established patterns, then run the test suite against the configured database.

item.aggregated_output:
src/queries.mjs-3-// Order summary: combines order + account + line items + invoice info
src/queries.mjs-4-export async function getOrderSummary(db, orderId) {
src/queries.mjs-5-  await ensureSchema(db);
src/queries.mjs-6-  const order = await db.collection("orders").findOne({ orderId });
src/queries.mjs-7-  if (!order) return null;
src/queries.mjs:8:  const account = await db.collection("accounts").findOne({ accountId: order.accountId });
src/queries.mjs-9-  const invoice = await db.collection("invoices").findOne({ orderId });
src/queries.mjs-10-  return {
src/queries.mjs-11-    orderId: order.orderId,
src/queries.mjs-12-    status: order.status,
src/queries.mjs-13-    totalCents: order.totalCents,
--
src/accounts.mjs-1-import { ensureSchema } from "./schema.mjs";
src/accounts.mjs-2-
src/accounts.mjs-3-export async function createAccount(db, data) {
src/accounts.mjs-4-  await ensureSchema(db);
src/accounts.mjs:5:  const result = await db.collection("accounts").insertOne(data);
src/accounts.mjs-6-  return { _id: result.insertedId, ...data };
src/accounts.mjs-7-}
src/accounts.mjs-8-
src/accounts.mjs-9-export async function getAccount(db, accountId) {
src/accounts.mjs:10:  return db.collection("accounts").findOne({ accountId });
src/accounts.mjs-11-}
src/accounts.mjs-12-
src/accounts.mjs-13-export async function updateAccount(db, accountId, data) {
src/accounts.mjs:14:  await db.collection("accounts").updateOne({ accountId }, { $set: data });
src/accounts.mjs:15:  return db.collection("accounts").findOne({ accountId });
src/accounts.mjs-16-}

item.text: The accounts validator now accepts the optional four-value enum, while the model persists it on both create and update; the order summary projects it from the account. I’m running the acceptance suite now.

item.aggregated_output:
npm notice run build-bench-mongo-schema-evolution-enterprise@1.0.0 test
npm notice run node tests/acceptance.test.mjs
Build-Bench enterprise acceptance passed: mongo

item.text:
Implemented preferred payment method support.

- `accounts` schema validates optional `credit_card`, `wire_transfer`, `ach`, or `paypal`.
- Account create/update persist the field.
- Order summaries include `preferredPaymentMethod`.

`npm test` passes.
